title: Calappa yatay
intro: Calappa yatay, also known as the Indian calabash, is a type of medicinal plant native to tropical regions. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial and fungal infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Calappa yatay include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Calappa yatay is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
